title: Convert an Array Into a 2D Array With Conditions

testcases.input: nums = [1,3,4,1,2,3,1] Output: [[1,3,4,2],[1,3],[1]] Explanation: We can create a 2D array that contains the following rows: - 1,3,4,2 - 1,3 - 1 All elements of nums were used, and each row of the 2D array contains distinct integers, so it is a valid answer. It can be shown that we cannot have less than 3 rows in a valid array.
testcases.expectedOutput: [[1,3,4,2],[1,3],[1]] Explanation: We can create a 2D array that contains the following rows: - 1,3,4,2 - 1,3 - 1 All elements of nums were used, and each row of the 2D array contains distinct integers, so it is a valid answer. It can be shown that we cannot have less than 3 rows in a valid array.

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,3,4] Output: [[4,3,2,1]] Explanation: All elements of the array are distinct, so we can keep all of them in the first row of the 2D array.
testcases.expectedOutput: [[4,3,2,1]] Explanation: All elements of the array are distinct, so we can keep all of them in the first row of the 2D array.
